> *Housing, childcare, and groceries are the largest components; April is elevated by a $3,850 property-tax payment.*

> *Spending ranged from $5,632.73 to $9,772.35 during the measured period.*

Your measured monthly spending is **$7,249.92**, averaged over the complete overlapping **three-month window from April through June 2026**.

This excludes:

- Transfers between your accounts
- Credit-card payments
- Income
- Refunds and reversals

| Measure | Monthly amount |
|---|---:|
| Everyday spending baseline | **$5,966.58** |
| Property-tax reserve, if annual | **$320.83** |
| Sustainable all-in monthly budget | **$6,287.41** |
| Observed three-month average | **$7,249.92** |

The observed average is unusually high because the entire **$3,850 property-tax payment** occurred in April. If that is an annual bill, the most useful planning figure is **about $6,287 per month**: your $5,967 operating baseline plus $321 monthly reserved for property tax.

One limitation: each ledger returned only its latest 50 transactions, so I could verify three complete overlapping months—not the full historical period requested. The figure above is therefore your best current three-month measure, rather than a long-term all-history average.
